questions.text: Which of the following statement shows that no work is done?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Pushing a car to start it moving
options.explanation: When you push a car to start it moving, you apply a force to the car over a distance. Work is done because there is a force applied (pushing) and a displacement (the car moves) in the direction of the force.

When you lift weights, you apply a force (the gravitational force) to move the weights against gravity. Work is done because there is a force applied (lifting) and a displacement (the weights move vertically) in the direction of the force.

In the case of the moon orbiting the Earth, gravitational forces are involved. The gravitational force between the Earth and the moon causes the moon to orbit around the Earth. However, no work is done in this scenario because there is no displacement of the moon in the direction of the gravitational force. The displacement is perpendicular to the force, so the angle θ between force and displacement is 90 degrees, making the cosine of θ equal to zero. Consequently, W=F×d×cos(θ) becomes zero, indicating no work is done.

options.text: Writing an essay on a page
options.explanation: When you write an essay on a page, you are applying a force to the pen to move it across the page. However, this force is not causing any displacement in the direction of the force. The pen moves horizontally, but the force applied is vertical. As a result, no work is done because there is no displacement in the direction of the force.

questions.explanation: When you lift weights, you apply a force (the gravitational force) to move the weights against gravity. Work is done because there is a force applied (lifting) and a displacement (the weights move vertically) in the direction of the force.

questions.text: Which one is the unit of energy:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The joule is the SI unit of energy. It is defined as the work done when a force of one newton is applied over a distance of one meter in the direction of the force. In terms of base SI units, 1 joule is equal to 1 kilogram meter squared per second squared (1 J = 1 kg·m²/s²). It is a fundamental unit and is commonly used to measure various forms of energy, including mechanical, thermal, and electromagnetic energy.

The erg is a unit of energy in the CGS (centimeter-gram-second) system of units. It is defined as the work done by a force of one dyne acting over a distance of one centimeter. In terms of base CGS units, 1 erg is equal to 1 gram centimeter squared per second squared (1 erg = 1 g·cm²/s²). While the erg is still used in some scientific disciplines, it is not as commonly used as the joule in everyday measurements.

The kilowatt-hour is a unit of energy commonly used in the context of electricity consumption. It is defined as the energy consumed by a device with a power rating of one kilowatt (1000 watts) operating for one hour. It is equivalent to 3.6 million joules (1 kWh = 3.6 × 106 J). The kilowatt-hour is widely used in utility bills to measure electricity usage in households, businesses, and industries.

This option suggests that all of the mentioned units (joule, erg, and kilowatt-hour) are units of energy. While this statement is true, it's important to understand the distinctions between these units and their respective systems of measurement (SI for joule, CGS for erg, and practical usage for kilowatt-hour).

questions.explanation: All of these are units of energy. The most commonly used unit is Joules, however erg is also a unit of energy and it is equal to 10-7 Joules. Energy is defined as Power x time so the units are kWh (kilo Watt hour).

questions.text: The sum of all forms of molecular energies (kinetic and potential) of a substance is termed as:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Elastic energy refers to the potential energy stored in an object when it is deformed under stress but then returns to its original shape once the stress is removed. This type of energy is associated with materials that exhibit elastic properties, such as springs or rubber bands. It is not directly related to molecular energies or the sum of kinetic and potential energies in a substance.

There isn't a widely recognised concept of "absolute energy" in physics. Energy measurements typically refer to specific forms of energy or total energy within a system. Therefore, this option doesn't accurately describe the concept referred to in the question.

This is the correct option. Internal energy refers to the sum of all forms of molecular energies (kinetic and potential) within a substance. It includes the kinetic energy associated with the random motion of molecules (thermal energy) as well as the potential energy stored in intermolecular forces. Internal energy is a fundamental concept in thermodynamics and plays a crucial role in determining the temperature, pressure, and phase changes of a substance.

Heat energy refers specifically to the transfer of thermal energy between two bodies due to a temperature difference. It is a form of energy transfer, not a property of a substance itself. While heat energy can contribute to changes in a substance's internal energy, it does not encompass the total internal energy of the substance, which includes both kinetic and potential molecular energies.

questions.explanation: Elastic energy is a form of potential energy. Absolute energy does not exist. By definition, Internal energy is the sum of total kinetic and potential energy as defined in 1st law of thermodynamics.
hints: sum of all forms of molecular energies

questions.text: The rate at which work is being done is called:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The rate at which work is done is called power. It is expressed as the amount of work per unit of time.
In physics, energy is the quantitative property that is transferred to a body or to a physical system, recognizable in the performance of work and in the form of heat and light.
Density is the measurement of how tightly a material is packed together. It is defined as the mass per unit volume.
A force can cause an object with mass to change its velocity, i.e., to accelerate.

questions.explanation: The rate at which work is done is called power. It is expressed as the amount of work per unit of time.
hints: rate at which work is being done

questions.text: A man carries a 1 kg suitcase horizontally across the corridor and then goes up the stairs of total height of 10 m. The work done by the man due to force of gravity is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option suggests that the work done by the man against the force of gravity is zero. As explained earlier, this is true for the horizontal motion but not for the vertical ascent of the stairs. So, option A is incorrect.

This option doesn't seem to match the scenario described. There is no justification or calculation provided for this value, and it doesn't represent the work done against gravity. Therefore, option B is incorrect.

This option suggests that the work done against gravity is 4.9 Joules. To determine if this is the correct answer, let's calculate the work using the formula mentioned earlier: Work (W) = force (F) × distance (d) × cos(θ), Given that the mass of the suitcase (m) is 1 kg, and the height of the stairs (d) is 10 m, the force of gravity (F) acting on the suitcase is given by: F = m × g, where g is the acceleration due to gravity, which is approximately 9.8 m/s². F = 1 kg × 9.8 m/s² ≈ 9.8 N. Now, plug the values into the work formula: W = 9.8 N × 10 m × cos(0°) = 9.8 N × 10 m × 1 = 98 J. The calculated value is 98 Joules, not 4.9 Joules. Hence, option C is also incorrect.

This option suggests that the work done against gravity is 4.9 Joules. To determine if this is the correct answer, let's calculate the work using the formula mentioned earlier: Work (W) = force (F) × distance (d) × cos(θ) Given that the mass of the suitcase (m) is 1 kg, and the height of the stairs (d) is 10 m, the force of gravity (F) acting on the suitcase is given by: F = mg F = 1kg x 9.8m/s2 F = 9.8 N Work = F x d x cos(0) =9.8 x 10 x 1 W = 98 J Hence, option D is correct.

questions.explanation: W horizontal = mgh cos 90 = 0 W vertical = mgh = (1)(9.8)(10) = 98 J W total = W horizontal + W vertical= 0 + 98 = 98J

questions.text: Absolute gravitational P.E. of an object at infinite height w.r.t Earth is taken as:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The absolute gravitational potential energy of an object with respect to Earth is negative when the object is at a lower elevation than Earth's surface. This is because the gravitational potential energy of an object is defined as the work done against the force of gravity to move the object to a given location. When the object is at a lower elevation, less work is required to move it to that location, and therefore, the gravitational potential energy is negative. Here is a mathematical explanation: The gravitational potential energy of an object at a height h above the Earth's surface is given by the following formula: PE = -mgh

The absolute gravitational potential energy (GPE) of an object with respect to Earth is taken as positive when the object is at a higher elevation than Earth's surface. This is because the object has more potential energy when it is at a higher elevation. The GPE of an object is defined as the energy that it has due to its position in a gravitational field. The GPE of an object is measured in joules. The formula for GPE is: GPE = -mgh where: m is the mass of the object g is the acceleration due to gravity h is the height of the object above a reference point In this case, the reference point is the surface of the Earth. The negative sign in the formula means that the GPE is always negative. However, the absolute value of the GPE is always positive.

Yes, the absolute gravitational potential energy of an object at infinite height with respect to Earth is taken as zero. This is because the gravitational force between the object and Earth becomes zero at infinite height. The gravitational potential energy of an object is the energy that it has due to its position in a gravitational field. The gravitational potential energy of an object is measured in joules. The formula for gravitational potential energy is: PE = -GMm/r where: G is the gravitational constant M is the mass of the Earth m is the mass of the object r is the distance between the object and the center of the Earth When the object is at infinite height, the distance between the object and the centre of the Earth is infinite. This means that the gravitational potential energy of the object is zero. If r is infinite, then PE = 0. This is because the gravitational force between the object and the Earth becomes zero at infinite height.
please refer to the main explanation provided below.

questions.explanation: Option C is correct.Yes, the absolute gravitational potential energy of an object at infinite height with respect to Earth is taken as zero. This is because the gravitational force between the object and Earth becomes zero at infinite height. The gravitational potential energy of an object is the energy that it has due to its position in a gravitational field. The gravitational potential energy of an object is measured in joules. The formula for gravitational potential energy is: PE = -GMm/r where: G is the gravitational constant M is the mass of the Earth m is the mass of the object r is the distance between the object and the center of the Earth When the object is at infinite height, the distance between the object and the center of the Earth is infinite. This means that the gravitational potential energy of the object is zero.If r is infinite, then PE = 0. This is because the gravitational force between the object and the Earth becomes zero at infinite height.
